text: The sequence validator should reject a `STEPLIST` entry missing `from` or `to`, because the panel renders the who->who chip only when both are present and the authoring guide treats from/to as required, yet validate.mjs only counts `title:` keys and passes an entry with no from/to.

The sequence validator should flag an unbalanced activation bar — an opening `->>+` (or `activate`) with no matching closing `-->>-` (or `deactivate`) — which is a real Mermaid render-breaker, yet validate.mjs passes a diagram that opens an activation and never closes it.

The sequence validator should flag a `STEPLIST` entry whose `kind` contradicts its arrow (e.g. `kind:"return"` on a solid sync `->>` message, or `kind:"sync"` on a dashed `-->>` return), since the guide says kind must match the arrow, but validate.mjs never cross-checks kind against the diagram arrows.

The sequence validator should reject a `STEPLIST` entry missing the `kind` field, because the panel and legend rely on sync/return/async classification and the authoring guide lists kind as part of the required entry shape, yet validate.mjs passes an entry that omits kind entirely.

references/components.md must show how to model a retry-with-backoff interaction (a `loop` whose exit/`break` condition and per-attempt delay are expressed) so a retry/backoff use case maps cleanly to numbered steps; today the guide's only `loop` example is `loop every 30s` with no exit condition, backoff, or break guidance.

SUBJECTIVE (human-only): each `STEPLIST` entry's narrative should faithfully describe the message at its 1:1 position (right who->who, right intent) rather than being reordered or mislabeled, which the count-only parity check cannot verify and needs human reading.

SUBJECTIVE (human-only): the single `crit:true` step should mark the genuine trust-boundary crossing or irreversible write, a semantic judgment about which message matters most that no deterministic check can make.
